ops.title: Fleet AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/install-binary.sh/settings.json/.gitignore are cc-guides-rendered and CI-OWNED — edit fragments only, push, CI renders+commits artifacts+lock; NEVER render locally (repo-creation flows excepted); provenance in cc-guides.lock, ar

ops.body:
As of 2026-07-10 (v3.1 lock model), fleet repos render `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, install-binary.sh where adopted, and (migrated repos) `.claude/settings.json` from `.claude/fragments/<target-path>/` layout dirs. md/sh artifacts carry only a version-free `<!-- GENERATED … -->` marker and json artifacts nothing; ALL provenance (binary version, full-sha pins, managed-target registry) lives in `.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock`.

**Why:** an edit to the rendered artifact alone passes locally and pushes fine, then the next daily re-render composes from the unchanged fragments and lands a `chore: re-render guides` commit that silently reverts it (observed live in the v1 era: a 31-repo re-stamp "succeeded" 29/31, only 14 survived). The `Guides` check re-composes from the lock's pins and reds on drift.

**How to apply:** edit the local `*.fragment.*` files (never the artifact), `cc-guides render`, commit fragments + artifacts + lock in ONE commit; verify against main@origin after Guides CI settles. Shared prose changes go in cc-skills `plugin/guides/` — each repo's daily cron picks them up ([[never-sweep-guides-fleet]]: no dispatch loops). In cc-skills itself a **pack**-fragment edit is TWO ships, because render resolves the `cc-skills` source from GitHub @main, not the working tree: ship the pack fragments first, plain-render (re-resolves @main to the pushed sha), then ship AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md + lock. `cc-guides render --source cc-skills=plugin/guides` previews local pack edits but stamps `spec = "plugin/guides"` / `commit = "local"` into the lock — never committable (proven 2026-07-21). Settings deltas go in `settings-overrides.fragment.json` (union-merge cannot remove a base entry — removals require moving the key out of the shared fragment). cc-context plugin ≥0.9.0 enforces: PreToolUse blocks direct edits of managed artifacts. See [[cc-guides-v3-architecture]]. Related: [[model-routing-convention]], [[shared-working-copy-concurrency]].

Since 2026-07-20: the root **`.gitignore` is a managed artifact fleet-wide (all 44 lock-carrying repos)** — cc-guides ≥0.1.42 has a `gitignore` kind (the `.gitignore` dotfile name IS the dispatch extension), composed from cc-skills `plugin/guides/gitignore/` fragments (base + per-layer + docs) with the repo-local `gitignore-local` fragment LAST so negations win. New ignore rules go in the pack fragments (fleet-wide via daily crons) or the repo's `gitignore-local.fragment.gitignore` — never the rendered file. gd-build artifacts are ignored via any-depth `.gd-build/` in the docs fragment. Full policy record: cc-notes note eb3d60cd in cc-skills.

Since 2026-07-11: rendered settings.json carries **no `hooks` key anywhere in the fleet** — capt-hook registration is plugin-canonical ([[hook-multi-fire-was-multi-source-registration]]); a guard test in cc-skills (`plugins/repo-bootstrap/tests/test_guides_fragments.py`) reds any guides fragment that re-grows hooks or a capt-hook string.
